edits.description: JavaCalculator.computeTotal() widens its return type from int to long. Only the .java file is touched - OrderService.kt (one hop) and MainActivity.kt (two hops) both call it transitively without any signature declared in their own source, so this is D2's 'a Java signature change forces Kotlin recompile' case, and the bound is tight enough to fail if either Kotlin dependent is left stale.

edits.description: Change NodeRenderer.render()'s body, on the Java side of the cycle. No signature moves, so no Kotlin class' bytecode may change - the bound catches a regression where the Java pass' conservative full-Kotlin-recompile starts emitting different Kotlin bytecode.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'QB_CYCLIC_J_MARKER_V2' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: NodeRenderer.render() widens its return type from String to CharSequence. Only the .java file is touched, but TreeNode.describe() infers its return type from that call, so TreeNode's own ABI changes and every caller of describe() (MainActivity) plus the Java override (JavaLeafNode, which now needs a covariant bridge) must be recompiled. If the Kotlin side were left stale it would still call render()Ljava/lang/String; - a NoSuchMethodError at run time - so this edit is the never-stale check for the Java->Kotlin direction of the cycle.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'QB_CYCLIC_JSIG_MARKER_V2' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: TreeNode gains a required depth constructor parameter. Java has no default-parameter sugar, so the Java subclass (JavaLeafNode, whose super call and describe() body both change) must move in the same changeset, as must the Kotlin caller MainActivity - the mirror of edit 03, driving the change Kotlin->Java across the cycle.

assertions.name: behavioral_marker
assertions.detail: marker 'QB_CYCLIC_KSIG_MARKER_V2' found in recompiled class bytes

edits.description: Replace the bundled arm64-v8a libnativestub.so with a rebuilt binary. ChangeClassifier has no packaging story for a changed native library - it's an UNSUPPORTED_FILE_CHANGED fallback (see ChangeClassifierTest's 'native library under jniLibs falls back honestly'), so this must route to a full Gradle rebaseline rather than be served as a quick build (D7's '.so change routes to REBASELINE').

edits.description: LineSeparator gains a public method, so the Java ABI moves and every Kotlin source in the module is recompiled (the conservative path - see IncrementalCompiler.kotlinFilesToCompile). Adding a method cannot change any caller's bytecode, so the recompiled SET stays small; what this edit measures is the wall-clock cost of that conservatism on a 73-Kotlin-file module, against edit 01's fast path.
